name: acct_mgmt_policy_demo
description: Policy-prose case modeled on Information_System_Account_Management_Policy_USD20240315.docx — policy docs are LESS host-dense than SSPs/architecture docs. Pins the lower-token-count, lower-confidence floor: a generic AC-2 procedure should still emit role + lifecycle vocabulary but not invent hostnames.

fixture_docs.title: Information System Account Management Policy USD20240315
fixture_docs.text:
1.0 PURPOSE

This policy establishes the requirements for creating, modifying, disabling, and removing information-system accounts on Example information systems. It implements NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls in the AC-2 family.

2.0 SCOPE

Applies to all Example information systems holding or processing federal information, including but not limited to FedRAMP-authorized cloud services and on-premises systems hosting CUI.

3.0 ROLES

  * Information System Owner (ISO) -- authorizes account creation
  * Information System Security Officer (ISSO) -- approves privileged accounts
  * System Administrator -- executes creates/modifies/disables in target system
  * Account Holder -- end user with assigned credentials

4.0 ACCOUNT LIFECYCLE

4.1 Initial provisioning requires a documented business justification approved by the ISO.
4.2 Privileged accounts require ISSO approval and quarterly re-attestation.
4.3 Inactive accounts must be disabled within 35 days; accounts of separated personnel within 24 hours of HR notification.
4.4 All account creation, modification, and removal events shall be logged centrally.
